pattern: Scaling servings to units of 25 mL gives a small recursive probability that averages four sub-states, and because the answer rushes to one for large inputs a fixed cap keeps it tractable.
hints: Every operation moves the soups in multiples of 25 mL, so divide by 25 and work in whole units to shrink the state space.
The probability for a state is the average of the four next states, with a half credit when both soups empty together and full credit when only A empties.
Memoize the recursion on remaining units of A and B; for very large n the probability is so close to one that you can return one once n passes a few thousand.

edge_inputs.description: zero millilitres empties both at once for half credit
smallest positive amount below one full serving
exactly one serving of A in the first move
non-multiple of 25 must round up to a full unit
moderate amount still far from the limit
large input where the probability is effectively one
value near the practical cap should already be one

stress.description: random small to moderate amounts where the probability genuinely varies
stress.note: kept under the cap so the value is a real probability rather than a saturated one
